evidence_summary: Batch normalization can be applied to stabilize deep neural network training by normalizing the activities of the neurons, which reduces the training time in feedforward neural networks.
Weight normalization can also help stabilize training by self-stabilizing the norm of the gradients, making optimization more robust to the value of the learning rate.

Layer normalization, a variant of batch normalization, can be used to normalize the summed inputs to the neurons in a layer on a single training case, which can help reduce the effect of internal covariate shift.
